# Exode SDK — full context for AI assistants

This file is a self-contained reference for the `@exode-team/sdk` package.
Feed it to an LLM (ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, etc.) so the model can write
correct integration code for the Exode platform without any extra context.

Package version: **0.1.0**
Repository: https://github.com/exode-team/exode-sdk
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@exode-team/sdk

---

## 1. Package overview

`@exode-team/sdk` is the official SDK for Exode (an EdTech SaaS platform
for online schools). It ships two independent modules:

| Import | Purpose | Runtime |
|--------|---------|---------|
| `@exode-team/sdk/api` | Server-side REST client `ExodeAPI` for `/saas/v2/*` | Node.js >= 18 |
| `@exode-team/sdk/miniapp` | Client-side bridge for iframe mini apps | Browser |
| `@exode-team/sdk/miniapp/react` | React hooks on top of the bridge | Browser + React >= 17 |

Requirements:
- Node.js >= 18
- TypeScript >= 5.7 (optional; types ship with the package)
- React >= 17 — optional peer dependency, only needed for `miniapp/react`

Install:

```bash
npm install @exode-team/sdk
```

Or via CDN (for static pages without a bundler):

```html
<script src="https://cdn.exode.biz/sdk/v0.1.0/miniapp.global.js"></script>
<!-- Global: ExodeMiniAppSDK -->
```

Important: there is no root export for `@exode-team/sdk` — always import
from a specific subpath.

---

## 2. API Client (`@exode-team/sdk/api`)

Server-side HTTP client for the REST endpoints under `/saas/v2/*`.
Server-only (the token must never ship to the browser).

### 2.1 Constructor

```ts
import { ExodeAPI, ExodeAPIError } from '@exode-team/sdk/api'

interface ExodeAPIConfig {
  sellerId: number       // required -> header Seller-Id
  schoolId: number       // required -> header School-Id
  token: string          // required -> Authorization: Bearer <token>
  baseUrl?: string       // default 'https://api.exode.biz/saas/v2'
  timeout?: number       // ms, default 30000
}

const exodeApi = new ExodeAPI({
  sellerId: 1,
  schoolId: 1,
  token: process.env.EXODE_TOKEN!,
})
```

The constructor throws an `Error` if:
- `token` is missing
- `sellerId` or `schoolId` is missing

### 2.2 Low-level request

Used internally by resource methods; you usually don't need it directly:

```ts
exodeApi.request<T>(method: string, path: string, options?: {
  body?: unknown
  query?: Record<string, string | number | undefined>
}): Promise<T>
```

### 2.3 Resource: `school.user`

```ts
// CREATE — at least one login (email/phone/tgId/extId) is required
exodeApi.school.user.create(params: {
  email?: string | null
  phone?: string | null
  tgId?: number | null
  extId?: string | null
  banned?: boolean
  profile?: {
    firstName?: string | null
    lastName?: string | null
    bdate?: string | null         // ISO
    sex?: 'Ufo' | 'Women' | 'Men' | null
    role?: 'Student' | 'Tutor' | 'Parent' | null
    contact?: {
      phone?: string | null
      email?: string | null
      messengerUrl?: string | null
    }
  }
}): Promise<UserEntity>

// UPDATE
exodeApi.school.user.update(
  userId: number,
  params: UserUpdateParams,      // same shape as create
): Promise<UserEntity>

// UPSERT — creates or updates by login
exodeApi.school.user.upsert(params: UserCreateParams):
  Promise<{ user: UserEntity; isCreated: boolean }>

// FIND — search by login/tgId/extId (sent as query params)
exodeApi.school.user.find(params: {
  login?: string
  tgId?: number
  extId?: string
}): Promise<UserEntity | null>

// STATE — arbitrary per-user key-value storage
exodeApi.school.user.getState(
  userId: number,
  key: 'CustomField' | 'UtmSignupParams' | 'PersonalInfoFilled',
): Promise<unknown>

exodeApi.school.user.setState(
  userId: number,
  key: UserStateKey,
  value: unknown,
): Promise<{ set: boolean }>

// AUTH TOKEN — creates a session and returns session.token for auto-login
exodeApi.school.user.createAuthToken(params: {
  userId: number
  forceCreate?: boolean
}): Promise<{ session: SessionEntity; isCreated: boolean }>
```

Use the returned `session.token` as the `?___uat=<token>` query parameter
on a school URL — Exode will auto-authenticate the user. This is the
canonical way to sign a user in from an external system (including
Telegram Mini Apps).

### 2.4 Resource: `school.group`

```ts
// Add members
exodeApi.school.group.addMembers(
  groupId: number,
  userIds: number[],
): Promise<GroupMemberCreateResult>

// Remove members
exodeApi.school.group.removeMembers(
  groupId: number,
  userIds: number[],
): Promise<{ affected: number }>
```

### 2.5 Resource: `school.queryExport`

Async exports: `generate` returns a UUID; poll the result with `getResult`.

```ts
import { QueryExportType, QueryExportFormat } from '@exode-team/sdk/api'

// Start — returns a WorkflowExecutionEntity with uuid
const { uuid } = await exodeApi.school.queryExport.generate({
  type: QueryExportType.GroupMemberFindMany,
  format: QueryExportFormat.Csv,
  filter: { groupId: 42 },
})

// Poll result
const result = await exodeApi.school.queryExport.getResult(uuid)
```

Enum values (these exact strings are sent to the API):

```ts
enum QueryExportType {
  GroupMemberFindMany                 = 'QUERY_EXPORT_TYPE_GROUP_MEMBER_FIND_MANY',
  CourseLessonPracticeAttemptFindMany = 'QUERY_EXPORT_TYPE_COURSE_LESSON_PRACTICE_ATTEMPT_FIND_MANY',
}

enum QueryExportFormat {
  Csv  = 'EXPORT_FORMAT_CSV',
  Xlsx = 'EXPORT_FORMAT_XLSX',
  Json = 'EXPORT_FORMAT_JSON',
}

enum WorkflowExecutionStatus {
  Waiting    = 'Waiting',
  Processing = 'Processing',
  Failed     = 'Failed',
  Canceled   = 'Canceled',
  Completed  = 'Completed',
}
```

### 2.6 API entity types

```ts
enum Language    { En = 'En', Ru = 'Ru', Uz = 'Uz', Qa = 'Qa' }
enum UserSex     { Ufo = 'Ufo', Women = 'Women', Men = 'Men' }
enum ProfileRole { Student = 'Student', Tutor = 'Tutor', Parent = 'Parent' }

interface ProfileEntity {
  id: number
  firstName: string | null
  lastName: string | null
  avatar: { id: number; small: string | null; medium: string | null; maximum: string | null }
  bdate: string | null
  sex: UserSex | null
  role: ProfileRole
  country: string | null
  city: string | null
  school: { id: string | null; city: number | null; class: string | null; name: string | null; speciality: string | null }
  official: boolean
  contact: { phone: string | null; email: string | null; messengerUrl: string | null } | null
  titleState: { /* manual / location / achievement titles — see SDK */ }
  createdAt: string           // ISO
  updatedAt: string | null
}

interface UserEntity {
  id: number
  uuid: string | null
  active: boolean
  activated: boolean
  banned: boolean
  domain: string | null
  email: string | null
  phone: string | null
  vkId: number | null
  tgId: number | null
  extId: string | null
  appleId: string | null
  language: Language | null
  timezone: number | null
  starsBalance: number
  lastOnlineAt: string | null
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string | null
  profile: ProfileEntity
}

interface SessionEntity {
  id: number
  uuid: string
  token: string               // put into the URL as ?___uat=<token>
  alive: boolean
  isOnline: boolean
  expireAt: string            // ISO
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string | null
}

interface WorkflowExecutionEntity {
  id: number
  uuid: string
  status: WorkflowExecutionStatus
  isCompleted: boolean
  createdAt: string
  updatedAt: string | null
}

interface WorkflowExecutionResult {
  total: number
  completed: number
  status: WorkflowExecutionStatus
  result: {
    fileUrl: string
    fileName: string
    fileSize: number
  } | null
}
```

### 2.7 API error handling

All failures are wrapped in `ExodeAPIError`:

```ts
class ExodeAPIError extends Error {
  readonly code: number          // HTTP status, or 0 (NetworkError) / 408 (Timeout)
  readonly errorCause: string    // 'EmailIsBusy', 'Unauthorized', 'Timeout', ...
  readonly details?: string
}

try {
  await exodeApi.school.user.create({ email: 'busy@example.com' })
} catch (e) {
  if (e instanceof ExodeAPIError) {
    if (e.errorCause === 'Unauthorized') { /* refresh token */ }
    if (e.errorCause === 'EmailIsBusy')  { /* show to user */ }
  }
}
```

API error response shape:
```json
{ "success": false, "code": 400, "cause": "EmailIsBusy", "message": "...", "error": "..." }
```

### 2.8 Typical scenario: auto-login a student

```ts
// 1. Find or create the user by Telegram id
const { user, isCreated } = await exodeApi.school.user.upsert({
  tgId: 123456789,
  profile: { firstName: 'Ivan', role: 'Student' },
})

// 2. Get a one-time session token
const { session } = await exodeApi.school.user.createAuthToken({ userId: user.id })

// 3. Build a deep link to the school
const url = `https://my-school.exode.biz?___uat=${session.token}`
```

---

## 3. MiniApp bridge (`@exode-team/sdk/miniapp`)

Client SDK for apps embedded in an iframe inside the Exode host app.
Provides two-way communication over `postMessage`.

Flow:
```
Exode App (host)      <-- postMessage -->      MiniApp (iframe)
```

Roles:
- **Host** (implemented inside the main Exode app) — sends context, receives commands.
- **Guest** (this SDK) — receives context, sends commands, listens to events.

### 3.1 ExodeMiniApp constructor

```ts
import { ExodeMiniApp } from '@exode-team/sdk/miniapp'

interface ExodeMiniAppConfig {
  appId: string              // required; validated by the host during handshake
  targetOrigin?: string      // default '*'; in production set the exact Exode origin
  timeout?: number           // ms, default 10000
}

const app = new ExodeMiniApp({ appId: 'my-app' })
```

Important: `ExodeMiniApp` must run **inside an iframe**. Calling `init()`
from a top-level window throws an error.

### 3.2 Handshake

```ts
const ctx: MiniAppContext = await app.init()
```

- Must be called exactly once — a second `init()` throws.
- If the host does not respond within `timeout` ms, the Promise rejects.
- Resolves with a `MiniAppContext` (see below).

### 3.3 Commands: MiniApp -> Host

```ts
// NAVIGATION (namespace: route)
await app.route.navigate(path: string, params?: Record<string, string>)
await app.route.back()

// UI (namespace: ui)
await app.ui.showSnackbar({
  message: string,
  type?: 'success' | 'error' | 'info',
})
await app.ui.setTabbarVisible(visible: boolean)
await app.ui.setHeaderVisible(visible: boolean)
await app.ui.close()
```

All commands return `Promise<void>`. If the bridge is not initialized
yet, the method throws `ExodeMiniApp is not initialized`.

### 3.4 Events: Host -> MiniApp

```ts
const unsubscribe = app.on<E extends MiniAppEventType>(
  event: E,
  handler: (payload: MiniAppEventMap[E]) => void,
): () => void

// Example
const off = app.on('theme:changed', (theme) => {
  document.body.className = theme.scheme
})
off()  // unsubscribe
```

Event map:

| Event | Payload |
|-------|---------|
| `theme:changed` | `MiniAppTheme` |
| `user:updated` | `MiniAppUser` |
| `school:updated` | `Record<string, unknown>` |
| `config:updated` | `MiniAppConfig` |
| `route:changed` | `{ path: string; params: Record<string, string> }` |
| `context:updated` | `Partial<MiniAppContext>` (auto-merged into cache) |
| `visibility:changed` | `{ visible: boolean }` |

`context:updated` is automatically merged into the internal context
cache — `app.getContext()` always returns the latest snapshot.

### 3.5 Accessing the context and cleanup

```ts
const ctx = app.getContext()   // MiniAppContext | null (null until init)

app.destroy()                  // closes the bridge and removes listeners
```

### 3.6 MiniApp types

```ts
type Platform = 'web' | 'native'

interface MiniAppContext {
  user: MiniAppUser
  school: Record<string, unknown>
  theme: MiniAppTheme
  platform: Platform
  config: MiniAppConfig
}

interface MiniAppUser {
  id: number
  uuid: string | null
  firstName: string | null
  lastName: string | null
  avatar: {
    id: number
    small: string | null
    medium: string | null
    maximum: string | null
  } | null
  email: string | null
  phone: string | null
  role: string                 // 'Student' | 'Tutor' | 'Parent' | ...
  language: string | null
}

interface MiniAppTheme { scheme: 'light' | 'dark' }

interface MiniAppConfig {
  isDesktop: boolean
  isMobile: boolean
  language: string
}

// Low-level postMessage envelope
interface BridgeMessage<T = unknown> {
  type: string
  requestId?: string
  payload?: T
  source: 'exode-host' | 'exode-miniapp'
}
```

### 3.7 Full vanilla-JS example

```ts
import { ExodeMiniApp } from '@exode-team/sdk/miniapp'

const app = new ExodeMiniApp({ appId: 'my-app' })

async function bootstrap() {
  const ctx = await app.init()

  document.body.className = ctx.theme.scheme
  document.getElementById('hello')!.textContent = `Hello, ${ctx.user.firstName}`

  app.on('theme:changed', (t) => { document.body.className = t.scheme })
  app.on('user:updated',  (u) => { console.log('user changed', u) })

  document.getElementById('save')!.addEventListener('click', async () => {
    await app.ui.showSnackbar({ message: 'Saved!', type: 'success' })
  })
}

bootstrap().catch((e) => console.error('MiniApp init failed', e))
window.addEventListener('beforeunload', () => app.destroy())
```

### 3.8 CDN usage (no bundler)

```html
<script src="https://cdn.exode.biz/sdk/v0.1.0/miniapp.global.js"></script>
<script>
  const app = new ExodeMiniAppSDK.ExodeMiniApp({ appId: 'my-app' })
  app.init().then((ctx) => {
    document.body.className = ctx.theme.scheme
  })
</script>
```

---

## 4. React hooks (`@exode-team/sdk/miniapp/react`)

A declarative layer on top of `ExodeMiniApp`. All hooks are reactive —
components re-render when host events fire.

### 4.1 Provider

```tsx
import { ExodeMiniAppProvider } from '@exode-team/sdk/miniapp/react'

<ExodeMiniAppProvider config={{ appId: 'my-app' }}>
  <App />
</ExodeMiniAppProvider>
```

Creates an `ExodeMiniApp`, performs the handshake, and subscribes to all
host events. Every hook below must be used **inside** this provider.

### 4.2 Hooks

```ts
useExodeApp():        { app: ExodeMiniApp | null; isReady: boolean; error: Error | null }
useExodeUser():       { user: MiniAppUser; isLoggedIn: boolean }
useExodeTheme():      { scheme: 'light' | 'dark'; isDark: boolean }
useExodeSchool():     { school: Record<string, unknown> }
useExodeConfig():     { isDesktop: boolean; isMobile: boolean; language: string; platform: Platform }
useExodeNavigation(): { navigate: (path, params?) => Promise<void>; back: () => Promise<void> }
useExodeUI():         {
  showSnackbar: (p: { message; type? }) => Promise<void>
  setTabbarVisible: (v: boolean) => Promise<void>
  setHeaderVisible: (v: boolean) => Promise<void>
  close: () => Promise<void>
}
```

### 4.3 Full React example

```tsx
import {
  ExodeMiniAppProvider,
  useExodeApp, useExodeUser, useExodeTheme, useExodeUI,
} from '@exode-team/sdk/miniapp/react'

function Content() {
  const { isReady, error } = useExodeApp()
  const { user } = useExodeUser()
  const { isDark } = useExodeTheme()
  const { showSnackbar } = useExodeUI()

  if (error)    return <div>Error: {error.message}</div>
  if (!isReady) return <div>Loading...</div>

  return (
    <div data-theme={isDark ? 'dark' : 'light'}>
      <h1>Hello, {user.firstName}</h1>
      <button onClick={() => showSnackbar({ message: 'Hi!', type: 'info' })}>
        Show snackbar
      </button>
    </div>
  )
}

export default function App() {
  return (
    <ExodeMiniAppProvider config={{ appId: 'my-app' }}>
      <Content />
    </ExodeMiniAppProvider>
  )
}
```

---

## 5. Cheat sheet: which module for which task

| Task | Import | Example |
|------|--------|---------|
| Create a user from your server | `@exode-team/sdk/api` | `exodeApi.school.user.create({ ... })` |
| Auto-login a user into a school | `@exode-team/sdk/api` | `createAuthToken` -> `?___uat=<token>` |
| Export group members | `@exode-team/sdk/api` | `queryExport.generate` + `getResult` |
| Build a widget embedded in Exode | `@exode-team/sdk/miniapp` | `new ExodeMiniApp({ appId })` |
| Same, with React | `@exode-team/sdk/miniapp/react` | `<ExodeMiniAppProvider>` + hooks |

## 6. Common pitfalls

- Do not import `@exode-team/sdk/api` in browser code — the token will leak.
- `ExodeMiniApp.init()` only works inside an iframe; calling it from a
  top-level window throws.
- Do not call `init()` twice on the same instance — it throws.
- `targetOrigin: '*'` is fine for local development only; in production
  pin the exact origin of the Exode host app.
- `QueryExportType` and `QueryExportFormat` are prefixed enums
  (`QUERY_EXPORT_TYPE_...`, `EXPORT_FORMAT_...`) — do not pass raw strings
  without the prefix.
- Required request headers: `Seller-Id`, `School-Id`,
  `Authorization: Bearer <token>` — `ExodeAPI` sets them automatically.

## 7. Links

- Docs: https://docs.exode.biz
- Exode API (REST): https://docs.exode.biz/ru/exode-api/setup
- Support: https://t.me/exode_support_biz
